recalls.Description: VESTA.DS has announced a recall of VST-brand wall-mounted gas boilers, specifically the Series VT, VR, and VR Plus models. The affected models include VRS-150, VRS-199, VRP-150, VRP-199, VRS Plus-150, VRS Plus-199, VRP Plus-150, VRP Plus-199, VTS-150, VTS-199, VTP-150, and VTP-199. These metal, rectangular units feature a white label on the side displaying the model number prominently at the top. The control panel may have 'VST' in white or 'VESTA.DS.Inc.' in gray printed on it.
recalls.Title: VESTA.DS Issues Recall for VST Brand Tankless Water Heaters Due to Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach VESTA.DS at 888-505-5525 from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email condensingwaterheater@realtimeresults.net, or visit www.recallrtr.com/condensingwaterheater or https://www.vestahws.com and select 'Recall' under the 'Documents/Support' tab.

Products.Name: VST Brand Gas-Fired Tankless Water Heaters
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 36,700 (In addition, about 3,500 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled VR Plus and VT Series Vesta Water Heaters
Recalled VR Series Vesta Water Heater
Model Number on the Label of the Recalled Water Heaters

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 33 reports of cracked exhaust ducts allowing exhaust fumes to leak out. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available in-store and online at Best Buy, Dick's Sporting Goods, Nebraska Furniture Mart, and ABT, as well as online at nordictrack.com and Amazon.com from November 2018 through April 2022, priced around $1,700.

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide (CO) poisoning due to potential exhaust duct cracking.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the water heater immediately and contact VESTA.DS for a free repair installation by a certified technician. If continued use is necessary, ensure functioning smoke and carbon monoxide alarms are present in the home.

recalls.metaDescription: VESTA.DS recalls VST tankless water heaters due to carbon monoxide poisoning risks. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Certain models of Camplux portable tankless water heaters are being recalled due to a potential fire hazard stemming from gas leaks at the regulator connection. The affected models include AY132, BD158, BW211, BW264, and BW422, manufactured on or before specific dates in 2021. These heaters are designed for outdoor use and are available in white, black, and silver/gray colors, featuring the 'Camplux' logo on the front. Product labels containing model details and serial numbers are located on the side of the units. Consumers are advised to photograph their product labels and send them to Camplux for verification of the recall and to receive a replacement gas regulator.
recalls.Title: Camplux Portable Tankless Water Heaters Recalled Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact The Home Appliances ("Camplux") at 844-538-7475 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email support@camplux.com. More details can be found online at www.thehomeappliances.com under the 'Recalls' section or directly at www.thehomeappliances.com/waterheaterrecall.

Products.Name: Camplux Portable Tankless Water Heater AY132

Injuries.Name: Camplux has received 19 reports of fire incidents involving the recalled water heaters. No injuries have been reported.

Importers.Name: The Home Appliances Inc., d/b/a Camplux, of North Charleston, South Carolina

Hazards.Name: Gas leak from the regulator connection, creating a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled heaters and contact The Home Appliances for a free repair kit, which includes a replacement gas regulator and installation instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Camplux recalls portable tankless water heaters due to fire hazards. Check if your model is affected and learn how to get a free repair kit.
